mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden unemployment rate increase above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate negative equity returns as investors price in earnings declines.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and credit demand, driving positive equity returns as corporate revenue forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in >50% of observed cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a sustained decline in industrial production signals supply-side contraction that outweighs rate effects, causing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with falling unemployment create an overheating economy signal, prompting central bank hawkishness that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized indicates demand stagnation, triggering immediate negative market repricing as earnings forecasts are downgraded across sectors.
failure_condition: Markets rally following weak GDP prints, producing positive 5-day returns in >55% of sub-1.5% growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic resilience and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue and generating positive forward equity returns through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation regimes, persistent CPI growth above 3% forces sustained higher policy rates, compressing equity multiples and driving negative returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: High CPI levels coincide with equity rallies over 60 days, yielding positive returns in >60% of triggered instances.
